leader: ['Antonella Attili', 'Enzo Caravale', 'Esa Danieli', 'Leo Gulotta', 'Mark Leonardi', 'Pupela Macchio', 'Agnes Nano', 'Leopold Trieste', 'Savatelli Casio', 'Nicola di Pinto', 'Roberta Reina', 'Nino Delozzo', 'Jacques Perhan', 'Philippe Noiret', 'Marina Giudice', 'Beatrice Palm', 'Roscoe Arbuckle', 'Junie Astol', 'Ignazio Balsamo', 'Brigitte Bardot', 'John Barrymore', 'Issa Barziza', 'Ingrid Bergman', 'Vilma Banki', 'Clara Karamay', 'Charlie Chaplin', 'Gary Cooper', 'Olivia de Havilland', 'Vittorio De Sica', 'Kirk Douglas', 'Errol Flynn', 'Brigitte Fossey', 'Jean Gabon', 'Clark Gable', 'Greta Garbo', 'Vittorio Gassman', 'Massimo Gilotti', 'Farley Granger', 'Cary Grant', 'Georgia Hale', 'Lawrence Harvey', 'Helen Hayes', 'Louis Jouve', 'Anna Magnani', 'Silvana Mangano', 'Marcello Mastroianni', 'Amedeo Nazari', 'Suzy Prim', 'Donna Reed', 'Jane Russell', 'Rosalind Russell', 'Yvonne Sanson', 'Maria Cher', 'Norma Sheila', 'Simon Signore', 'Alberto Sordi', 'James Stewart', 'Giuseppe Tornatore', 'Toto', 'Spencer Tracy', 'Claire Trevor', 'Rodolphe Valentino', 'Alida Valli', 'John Wayne']
description:
In a small town in southern Italy, the eccentric little boy Duoduo (played by Savatelli Casio) likes to watch movies, and even more likes to watch the projectionist Evert (played by Philippe Noiret) showing movies. He and Evert have become friends forever, and they find the joy of childhood life in film.
                                        
                                    In order to let more audiences see the movie, the kind-hearted Evert held an open-air movie. As a result, the film caught fire. Duoduo rescued Everda from the sea of fire, but Everda was blind. Duoduo became the only person in the town who could show movies. He succeeded Evert as the town's film projectionist.
                                        
                                    As Duoduo (played by Mark Leonardi) grows up, he falls in love with the banker's daughter Elena (played by Agnese Nano). The pure feeling of first love is as beautiful as heaven, but the vows of a young couple are cut off by Elena's father's obstruction. Duoduo goes to do military service, while Elena goes to college. The sad Duoduo left the town with the encouragement of Evert to pursue his dream in life...
                                        
                                    Thirty years later, Evert passed away. At this time, Duoduo was already a successful director. He returned to his hometown, saw the dilapidated Paradise Cinema, recalled the past, and was filled with regret. ...

title: Roman Holiday Roman Holiday
leader: ['Audrey Hepburn', 'Gregory Peck', 'Eddie Albert', 'Hartley Ball', 'Harcourt Williams', 'Margaret Rollins', 'Torio Caminati', 'Paolo Carlini', 'Claudio Ermelli', 'Paola Brumbuni', 'Rizzo Fredorizzo']
description:
Princess Anne (played by Audrey Hepburn) of a certain European country visits Rome. State affairs are troubled, but she is tired of red tape. One night, exhausted physically and mentally, she sneaks into the private sector to enjoy the night view, and meets newspaper reporter Joe (Gregory Peck) by chance. The two of them traveled hand in hand and were quite happy. The princess even visited Joe's home and spent the night there.
                                    
                                Unexpectedly, Joe accidentally discovered the true identity of the princess. He decided to concoct an exclusive news, so Joe and his friend, photographer Owen (Eddie Albert) took the princess on a trip to Rome and secretly took many photos of the princess's life. However, during the next interaction with the princess, Joe fell in love with the princess unknowingly. In order to protect the image of the princess, Qiao had no choice but to reluctantly give up the opportunity of success and give the photo to the princess.
                                    
                                After Princess Anne experienced a one-day vacation in Rome, she realized her responsibility to the country and resolutely returned to the embassy, resolutely abandoning love for the sake of her responsibility.

title: Scent of a Woman Scent of a Woman
leader: ['Al Pacino', 'Chris O'Donnell', 'James Rebhorn', 'Gabriel Anwar', 'Philip Seymour Hoffman', 'Richard Wincher', 'Bradley Whitford', 'Rochelle Oliver', 'Margaret Eginton', 'Tom Riis Farrell', 'Nicholas Sadler', 'Todd Lewis', 'Matt Smith', 'Gene Canfield', 'Frances Conroy']
description:
Charlie (Chris O'Donnell) is an ordinary middle school student. He witnessed a prank but didn't want to betray his friends, so he faced a difficult choice-either confess or be expelled from the school. And Sphalan (Al Pacino) is a retired military officer. Due to accidental blindness, his life fell from the peak of glory to the bottom.
                                    
                                Since the two people's lives intersected, the turning point of fate has gradually become clear. Sphalan planned to end his meaningless life, so accompanied by Charlie, he enjoyed the last trip of his life. He ate the most delicious food, bought a flashy car, raced on the narrow streets, lived in a luxurious suite, followed the scent of a strange woman's perfume, danced an elegant and sexy tango... After the blissful madness, he pointed a gun at his own head. However, Charlie's hoarse dissuasion changed everything, and the dawn gradually rose again in the old man's heart, and the two were as deeply in love as father and son.
                                    
                                Sphalan delivered a passionate speech in the school auditorium, saving Charlie's future and satirizing the school's hypocrisy. The two were reborn through mutual encouragement.

title: Defender 변호인
leader: ['Song Kang-ho', 'Kim Young-ae', 'Oh Dal-soo', 'Kwak Do-won', 'Im Si-wan', 'Song Young-chan', 'Jung Won-joong', 'Cho Min-ki', 'Lee Hang-na', 'Lee Sung-min', 'Cha Kwang-soo', 'Han Ki-joong', 'Shim Hee-seop', 'Jo Wan-ki', 'Lee Jung-eun']
description:
In 1978, Song Youshuo (played by Song Kanghao), who only had a high school degree, finally passed the judicial examination after years of hard work, and soon changed his career to become a lawyer after becoming a judge. He keenly sensed business opportunities from the latest policies and started his real estate agency business. Although he was ridiculed by his peers as a nightclub boy who hands out business cards anytime and anywhere, Youshuo did not feel dissatisfied and moved towards the goal in his heart step by step. He relied on the money he earned to enable his wife and children to live a prosperous life, and he also repaid the debt of conscience he owed to the hotel aunt seven years ago. Entering the 1980s, South Korea's struggle for democratization intensified. Youshuo didn't care about anything outside the window and continued to make money behind closed doors. However, the great changes in society no longer allow him to stay away. Zhenyu was arrested because the Busan Reading Federation to which he was affiliated was accused of being a left-wing society. He was also brutally abused and unfairly accused.
                                    
                                Although Woo-suk loves money, he values emotions more, and thus embarks on the road of defending democracy...

title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
leader: ['Daniel Radcliffe', 'Emma Watson', 'Rupert Grint', 'Alan Rickman', 'Maggie Smith', 'Tom Felton', 'Ian Hart', 'Richard Harris', 'John Hurt', 'Robbie Coltrane', 'Julie Waters', 'Bonnie White', 'John Cleese', 'Sean Biggerstaff']
description:
Harry Potter is an orphan who was fostered by his aunt since he was a child and was bullied. But on Harry's 11th birthday, he unexpectedly received the admission notice to Hogwarts College. Harry learned from the giant Hagrid who was sent to pick him up from the academy that this was a magic academy and learned about his life experience. It turned out that Harry's parents were both great magicians and both sacrificed their lives in the battle against Voldemort. Only Harry survived.
                                    
                                After Harry entered Hogwarts, he showed an unimaginable flying talent and was recommended by Professor McGonagall to join the Gryffindor Quidditch team. On the other hand, Harry discovered that a dark force seemed to be secretly growing in Hogwarts College, and the key to uncovering the mystery lay in the room guarded by the vicious three-headed dog.
                                    
                                Three good friends, Harry, Ron and Hermione, decided to find out.

title: Fight Club Fight Club
leader: ['Edward Norton', 'Brad Pitt', 'Helena Bonham Carter', 'Zach Grenier', 'Mit Love', 'Jared Leto', 'Ioan Bailey', 'Richmond Arquette', 'George Maguire']
description:
Jack (played by Edward Norton) is an employee of a large automobile company. He suffers from severe insomnia and is full of crisis and hatred for everything around him.
                                    
                                By chance, Jack met Taylor (Brad Pitt), a soap seller, a rogue hero full of rebelliousness, cruelty and violence. He moved into Taylor's dilapidated home because of a fire in his apartment. The two became good friends by chance and founded the "Fight Club", an underground organization that lets people vent their emotions by fighting with bare hands without wearing protective equipment.
                                    
                                The club attracted more and more people and gradually developed into a national underground organization, and Taylor also used his personal charm to attract blind believers. Members of the club were causing fights and wreaking havoc everywhere, and Taylor's behavior became increasingly crazier.
                                    
                                Jack became more and more intolerable of the current situation of "Fight Club" and Taylor's behavior. The two had a dispute and Taylor left him. However, Jack found that he could not escape Taylor's shadow wherever he went. He began to think: Who am I?

title: I'm not the god of medicine
leader: ['Xu Zheng', 'Wang Chuanjun', 'Yiwei Wei', 'Tan Zhuo', 'Zhang Yu', 'Yang Xinming', 'Wang Jiajia', 'Wang Yanhui', 'Jia Chenfei', 'Gong Beibi', 'Ning Hao', 'Li Naiwen', 'Yue Xiaojun', 'Wei Qing', 'Fu Guanming', 'Bharat Bhatti', 'Xili Tu', 'Zhang Haiyan', 'Zhu Gengyou']
description:
Cheng Yong (Xu Zheng), an ordinary middle-aged man who runs a health care product store, is frustrated and divorced. The arrival of the uninvited guest Lu Yiyi (played by Wang Chuanjun) allowed him to start a new business of buying medicines in India as a "purchasing agent". Although there were many difficulties, he found business opportunities on this "drug buying road" and out of control became the exclusive agent of Indian generic drugs for the treatment of chronic myelogenous leukemia. While making money, he also met several patients and their families, including Sihui (played by Tan Zhuo) who was forced to become a dancer to save her daughter, Pastor Liu (played by Yang Xinming) who spoke fluent "priestly" English, and "Huang Mao" (played by Zhang Yu) with a violent temper. They started a business together, and while their profits doubled, they were also surrounded by dangers. Cheng Yong's former brother-in-law, police officer Cao (played by Zhou Yiwei), is ordered to investigate the origin of generic drugs. Counterfeit drug dealer Zhang Changlin (played by Wang Yanhui) and Swiss genuine pharmaceutical representative (played by Li Naiwen) are also eyeing them, and the business gradually turns into a tug-of-war about redemption.
                                    
                                This film is adapted from the true story of Lu Yong, a patient with chronic myelogenous leukemia, who purchased anti-cancer drugs on his behalf.

title: Dead Poets Society Dead Poets Society
leader: ['Robin Williams', 'Robert Sean Leonard', 'Ethan Hawke', 'Josie Charles', 'Gail Hansen', 'Dylan Koosman', 'Aleron Ruggiero', 'James Waterston', 'Norman Lloyd', 'Kurtwood Smith', 'Cara Belwell', 'Leon Bonnar', 'George Martin', 'Jo Ofieri', 'Matt Carey', 'Kevin Cooney', 'Lara Flynn Ball', 'Alexandra Bowles', 'Melora Walters', 'Pamela Burrell', 'John Cunningham', 'Debra Mooney', 'Kurt Laettner', 'Keith Sneed', 'Jones Stickloris', 'Jamie Kennedy']
description:
Wilton Preparatory Academy is famous for its calm and dignified teaching style and high admission rate. As a graduating class student, the ideal is to enter a prestigious school. The arrival of John Keating (Robin Williams), a literature teacher in the new semester, is like a spring breeze, going against the seriousness and rigidity of traditional prestigious schools.
                                    
                                Keating took the students to listen to the sound of death in the school history building and reflect on the meaning of life; he let the boys read out their ideals on the green field; he encouraged the students to stand on the desks and overlook the world from a new perspective.
                                    
                                Professor Keating, Teacher Keating, Captain Keating, his education is like spring breeze and rain, moistening things silently and staying in everyone's heart...
